markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Add state recovery instructions for session compaction, interrupted MTs, failed builds, and validation reentry. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualStateRecoveryGuide leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualStateRecoveryGuide is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: State recovery guide is seeded and verified for compaction, interrupted MTs, failed builds, and validation reentry; proof and adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: Seeded state-recovery-guide page covers session compaction, interrupted microtasks, failed builds, validation reentry, receipt/DB state, and restartable no-context model use.
MT-206 targeted content test passed against real PostgreSQL: seeded state-recovery-guide covers session compaction, interrupted MTs, failed builds, and validation reentry.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.
Heisenberg read-only adversarial review returned PASS for MT-206; review noted this is content coverage, not semantic proof of every recovery instruction.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-206 requires a no-context UserManual state-recovery guide covering session compaction, interrupted microtasks, failed builds, and validation reentry, anchored to PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The implementation seeds a real state-recovery-guide page into managed PostgreSQL and proves coverage via a content test that queries the page back from the DB; the code delivers the MT behavior and aligns with current Master Spec 10.15.8.
findings: seed.rs:1164-1282 page_state_recovery_guide() defines a state_recovery page with exactly the four required scenarios as recovery-kind sections: 'After session compaction', 'After an interrupted microtask', 'After a failed build', 'Validation reentry'; each ties recovery to product authority (manual-toc, WP/MT contract, EventLedger receipts kernel_event_ledger, scoped tests, freshness verdicts, DEC-007).

seed.rs:185 registers page_state_recovery_guide() in seed_corpus(), so the page is actually seeded; seed.rs:1809-1840 ensure_seeded() upserts pages into real PostgreSQL via UserManualStore::upsert_page — a genuine durable resource write, not a mock.

tests/user_manual_content_tests.rs:449-483 mt206_state_recovery_guide_covers_contract_scenarios reads the page back from PG via get_page_by_slug, asserts page_kind=='state_recovery', >=4 sections, all four scenario substrings present, and every section kind 'recovery'.
tests/user_manual_support.rs:132-143 skip_if_no_pg! PANICS when PostgreSQL is absent (it does not silently skip), so a green test result is proof of a real PG round-trip.

Proof log ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/green-mt206-state-recovery-proof.log shows 'mt206_state_recovery_guide_covers_contract_scenarios ... ok' in 3.63s against external real PostgreSQL (pg-external-* logs; 127.0.0.1:5544 per commit fcf9bce3).

Spec alignment: master-spec-v02.194 10.15.8 (10-product-surfaces.md:8151-8157) requires a UserManualRecord to explain common failure modes and recovery steps, and 10.20 (:8545) mandates UserManual recovery panes for no-context model operation; the MT page directly satisfies this and carries spec_anchors 10.15.8 + 2.3.13.11 (seed.rs:1171).
No deferred-live escapes: grep of seed.rs found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/placeholder on the recovery page path.

Minor aside (non-blocking): the MT contract's scope.relevant_files still name legacy model_manual/* paths while the code lives under user_manual/* — this is the spec-sanctioned UserManual rename bridge (10.15.8), expected paperwork drift, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
